assertions.text: V1 was traveling westbound in lane 2 of a five-lane undivided roadway (two westbound lanes, two eastbound lanes, center turn lane).
V1 traversed the lane lines and entered lane 1 before departing the left side of the roadway.
V1 departed the left side of the roadway initiating a clockwise yaw (vehicle began rotating clockwise).
V1's left side impacted an overhead traffic signal pole.
V1 struck the overhead traffic signal pole and subsequently struck a concrete electric box (at least two distinct impacts).
The vehicle rotated rapidly clockwise around the pole as it split into two pieces.
The vehicle split into two pieces (front and rear sections) during the collision rotation.
The rear portion (rear box) came to rest in the center of the roadway.
The front portion continued westerly after separating and impacted a concrete electric box with its right side plane.
After impacting the concrete electric box, the front portion came to rest facing easterly.
The front portion impacted the concrete electric box with its right side.
The driver was ejected from V1.

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows pre-crash speed values with first_value [156.2](T2) km/h and last_value [122.4](T2) km/h and EDR vehicle and speed limit in [T1], but there is no GPS heading or lane data in the available channels to determine travel direction or which lane of a five-lane roadway V1 occupied; therefore lane and direction claims are unverifiable.

There are steering inputs with peak_signed_dev [ -91.5](T2) deg at [ -0.9](T2) s and a brake application at [ -0.5](T3) s, but no lane-position or camera/GPS lateral position channels are present in the telemetry to confirm traversal of lane lines or movement from lane 2 into lane 1 and departure of the left side of the roadway; therefore this assertion is unverifiable.

Crash pulse shows lateral cumulative delta-V final_value [4.0](T2) km/h with max at [0.21](T1) s and steering peak at [ -0.9](T2) s, but device-frame axes alignment to vehicle yaw is undocumented so the telemetry cannot confirm that the vehicle initiated a clockwise yaw; thus unverifiable under axis caution.

Telemetry records crash impulses: longitudinal delta-V final_value [7.7](T2) km/h and lateral delta-V max [4.3](T2) km/h, but there are no impact-direction sensors or external-object contact logs to identify that the left side impacted an overhead traffic signal pole; therefore unverifiable.

Detect_events reports two crash-pulse magnitudes: delta_v_long magnitude_kmh [7.7](T3) and delta_v_lat magnitude_kmh [4.3](T3), indicating impact pulses, but telemetry alone does not identify multiple distinct external impacts or their sequence (pole then concrete box); thus the claim of at least two distinct impacts is unverifiable from sensors provided.

The delta-V traces show rapid changes with final longitudinal delta-V [7.7](T2) km/h at [0.251](T1) s and lateral peak at [0.21](T1) s, but there is no sensor data describing rotation around a fixed external pole or structural splitting; vehicle-frame axis alignment is undocumented so the described rapid clockwise rotation and splitting around the pole is unverifiable.

EDR summary lists number of events recorded [3](T1) and crash-pulse data, but there are no sensors reporting vehicle integrity or separation into front/rear pieces, so the claim that the vehicle split into two pieces is unverifiable.

There are no position/final-rest GPS coordinates or lateral position channels in the telemetry to confirm where the rear portion came to rest; speed series shows last_value [122.4](T2) km/h before impact but rest positions are not recorded, so this is unverifiable.

Telemetry shows continued longitudinal delta-V and subsequent impulses (final longitudinal delta-V [7.7](T2) km/h), but there is no spatial impact logging to confirm the separated front struck a concrete electric box with its right side, so unverifiable.
No heading or final orientation data are present in the available channels to confirm the front portion came to rest facing easterly; thus unverifiable.
There is no per-side impact sensor or oriented contact data in the telemetry to confirm the front portion impacted the concrete box with its right side; therefore unverifiable.

EDR summary driver_belt_code is [1](T1) which indicates the driver belt was buckled (per EDR code), and the EDR records do not indicate an ejection event; additionally the vehicle records crash-pulse delta-V values (longitudinal final_value [7.7](T2) km/h and lateral final_value [4.0](T2) km/h) but no sensor evidence supports occupant ejection; because the narrative's specific claim of ejection is not supported and belt code suggests buckled, the ejection assertion is contradicted by the available telemetry.

result.rationale: Most narrative details about lane position, impacts with external objects, rotation direction around a pole, vehicle separation, resting positions and occupant ejection are not directly observable in the provided telemetry channels (speed, steering, delta-V). The crash pulse magnitudes are available ([7.7](T2) km/h longitudinal, [4.3](T2) km/h lateral per T3/T2), but spatial/visual claims are unverifiable; the ejection claim is contradicted by the driver_belt_code [1](T1) in the EDR.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
